_meta.description: Per-page index for case + story pages, so the cross-archive search can index narrative prose alongside per-archive manifests.

records.title: Belgian UFO Wave, 1989–90
records.desc: The Belgian UFO wave, November 1989 – April 1991 — triangular craft, F-16 scrambles, Belgian Air Force radar lock, public press conference by Colonel Wilfried De Brouwer. The most thoroughly documented military UAP engagement in modern NATO history.

records.title: Coyne helicopter near-miss
records.desc: U.S. Army Reserve UH-1H Iroquois piloted by Capt. Lawrence Coyne forced into an unexplained 1000 ft uncommanded climb after a near-collision with an unidentified cigar-shaped object near Mansfield, Ohio, 18 October 1973. Witnesses on the ground corroborated.

records.title: Gimbal, January 2015
records.desc: The Gimbal encounter, 20 January 2015, east coast of the United States. F/A-18F Super Hornet ATFLIR pod footage, declassified by the Department of Defense in April 2020. Offline-archived from AARO releases.

records.title: JAL Flight 1628 over Alaska
records.desc: Japan Air Lines cargo Boeing 747 freighter pursued by a UAP over interior Alaska for ~50 minutes. Captain Kenju Terauchi, multiple radar returns, FAA + NORAD review. Documented by FAA Anchorage ARTCC and the AARO 2024 Historical Record.

records.title: Phoenix Lights, March 1997
records.desc: The Phoenix Lights, 13 March 1997 — V-shaped craft observed by thousands across Arizona and the southwestern United States. Governor Symington's public confirmation a decade later. Offline-archived from DoD / Air Force releases.

records.title: AARO — All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of AARO — All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.aaro.mil.

records.title: Tehran 1976
records.desc: Imperial Iranian Air Force F-4 Phantom interception, 19 September 1976. Two F-4s scrambled; both reported weapons + radio systems disabled approaching the object. Documented in the DIA Defense Intelligence Agency Information Report dated 22 Sep 1976.

records.title: Nimitz "Tic-Tac", November 2004
records.desc: The USS Nimitz / USS Princeton encounters, 10–16 November 2004, off Baja California. Cmdr David Fravor, FLIR1 footage, AAW exercise off Catalina. Offline-archived from DoD / AARO releases.

records.title: Argentina · CEFAe — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Argentina · CEFAe, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.argentina.gob.ar.

records.title: Operação Prato, 1977–78
records.desc: Operation Saucer (Operação Prato) — the Brazilian Air Force investigation into the Chupa-Chupa flap on the Pará coast, September 1977 to January 1978. Captain Hollanda's 500-page report, declassified 2005.

records.title: Brazil — Força Aérea Brasileira / Arquivo Nacional — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Brazil — Força Aérea Brasileira / Arquivo Nacional, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.fab.mil.br.
records.agency: Brazil — Força Aérea Brasileira / Arquivo Nacional

records.title: Trindade Island, January 1958
records.desc: The Trindade Island incident, 16 January 1958 — Almirante Saldanha (NEE-15) deck witnesses photograph a Saturn-shaped UAP over the South Atlantic island. Photos certified by President Juscelino Kubitschek; the most famous government-authenticated UAP photographs in Brazilian history.

records.title: Varginha, January 1996
records.desc: The Varginha incident, 20 January 1996 — three witnesses, a stranded entity, an Army deployment, and a soldier's contested death. Offline-archived from Brazilian Air Force and Ministério da Defesa records.

records.title: Canada · LAC + Project Magnet — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Canada · LAC + Project Magnet, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.bac-lac.gc.ca.

records.title: Chile · CEFAA / SEFAA — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Chile · CEFAA / SEFAA, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.sefaa.cl.

records.title: GEIPAN — Phénomènes Aérospatiaux Non identifiés — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of GEIPAN — Phénomènes Aérospatiaux Non identifiés, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.cnes-geipan.fr.

records.title: Trans-en-Provence
records.desc: Renato Nicolaï observed a metallic disc land in his garden; soil + plant samples analysed at GEPAN-CNES and INRA-Toulouse over 30 months. Most-thoroughly studied physical-trace UAP case in scientific literature.

records.title: Italy · Aeronautica Militare — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Italy · Aeronautica Militare, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.aeronautica.difesa.it.

records.title: Roswell · Project Mogul
records.desc: The 8 July 1947 RAAF 'flying disc' press release and its successor explanations — Project Mogul (1994), Air Force GAO inquiry (1995), 'Case Closed' report (1997). Held at NARA in RG 341.

records.title: Socorro · Lonnie Zamora
records.desc: Patrolman Lonnie Zamora's daylight close-encounter observation of an egg-shaped object on landing legs, 24 April 1964, Socorro, NM. Project Blue Book case 8766; physical-trace investigation by Capt. Hector Quintanilla + Dr. J. Allen Hynek.

records.title: NARA — National Archives & Records Administration — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of NARA — National Archives & Records Administration, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from catalog.archives.gov.

records.title: NASA UAP Independent Study Team — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of NASA UAP Independent Study Team, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from science.nasa.gov.

records.title: The Kaikoura Lights
records.desc: Safe Air Argosy freighter sightings + RNZAF 16 mm film + Wellington ATC radar over the Kaikoura coast, 21–31 December 1978. Released by NZDF in 2010 under the Official Information Act.

records.title: New Zealand · NZDF — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of New Zealand · NZDF, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.nzdf.mil.nz.

records.title: Peru · OIFAA — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Peru · OIFAA, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.gob.pe.

records.title: Caso Manises
records.desc: TAE Supercaravelle JK297 forced to divert and land at Manises (Valencia) after two luminous objects paced the aircraft. Mirage F1 fighters scrambled. Full Ejército del Aire file released 1994.

records.title: Spain · Ejército del Aire — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Spain · Ejército del Aire, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from ejercitodelaire.defensa.gob.es.

records.title: Rendlesham Forest, Dec 1980
records.desc: The Rendlesham Forest incident, 26–28 December 1980 — RAF Woodbridge / RAF Bentwaters. Halt memo, witness depositions, DEFE 24/1948 file. Offline-archived from the UK National Archives.

records.title: UK · The National Archives — MoD UFO Files — story
records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of UK · The National Archives — MoD UFO Files, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.nationalarchives.gov.uk.
records.agency: UK · The National Archives — MoD UFO Files

records.desc: The mission, history, and public record of Uruguay · CRIDOVNI, as published by the source agency. Verbatim official-source text mirrored from www.fau.mil.uy.
